topic: Why being 'nice' is ruining your career
hook_summary: Stop being nice at work. It's costing you opportunities.

scenes.audio_text: Everyone says be nice at work. That's terrible advice.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel, 28-year-old man with short dark wavy hair, light stubble, dark polo shirt, medium close-up, locked-off camera, candid lighting, office break room, natural window light, documentary style.

scenes.audio_text: I got passed over for a promotion twice because I was too agreeable. My manager said I needed to 'push back more.'
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel, same man, over-the-shoulder shot from behind his laptop, soft ambient light from monitor, home office, messy desk with coffee mug, genuine frustrated expression.

scenes.audio_text: So I stopped agreeing to everything. I started saying 'no' to extra work that didn't help my goals.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel, walking on a city sidewalk, medium shot, slow push-in, golden hour sunlight, casual confident stride, looking ahead determined.

scenes.audio_text: Within 6 months, I got promoted and a 20% raise. Not because I worked harder, but because I stopped being a pushover.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel, sitting on a park bench, medium close-up, relaxed posture, natural shade lighting, slight smile, holding a coffee cup.

scenes.audio_text: Nice doesn't get you ahead. Respect does. Go earn it.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel, same park bench, locked-off camera, direct eye contact, confident expression, leaves slightly blowing in background.
